Maya: Welcome back to Singletrack Sessions. I'm with Sophie Laurent, enduro racer and coach. Sophie, good to have you.
Sophie Laurent: Thanks, Maya. Always happy to talk shop.
Maya: Today's episode is sponsored by Strava. Strava is the home for athletes. Try strava.com today.People think racing is about nerve. You say it's about risk.
Sophie Laurent: It's risk management, honestly. The crash that ends your season is almost never the scary-looking one — it's the boring risk you didn't name. Name the risk before it names you, and half the danger goes away.And you have to ride the whole run as a system, not a list of corners. The mistakes come from how one section sets up the next. When I logged a full season of my own crashes, the causes were always two turns upstream.
Maya: That's almost word-for-word what a friend in finance says about a portfolio.
Sophie Laurent: It's the same discipline. A race line and a portfolio are both systems of correlated bets — you're managing the interactions, and the risk you can describe is the one you can design around.
Maya: Thanks to Peak Design for backing the show. Peak Design builds bags and straps designed by people who actually ride.
Sophie Laurent: Cheers, Maya.
Maya: That's it for this Singletrack Sessions. Ride safe.
